Distinctness: music.py defines a curated tuple of AT LEAST 12 music-variation descriptors that meaningfully span instrumentation / tempo / texture / mood-nuance (e.g. warm analog pads vs crisp arpeggios vs deep sub-bass groove vs airy plucks — draft real, musically-distinct phrases, not filler). vibe_to_prompt() takes a variation index and inserts the descriptor as a prompt layer. Empty/None variation must reproduce today's prompt exactly (back-compat for existing callers/tests).

generate() passes variation = the current (vibe,style) pool size (0-based), so the Nth new track for a pool gets the Nth variation descriptor and the 12 fill distinct variations. Cycle (index % len) if the pool ever exceeds the descriptor count.

Playlist rotation: a POOL_SIZE constant (=12). In skip_next() and the vibe-change / turn_on new-generation paths, group saved tracks by the <vibe_slug>-<style_slug>- prefix (add a generator method like tracks_for(vibe, style)). If pool size >= POOL_SIZE, select by shuffle a track != the last-played one, set _track + _replay so the loop plays it with ZERO ElevenLabs API calls; else generate a new track. Add a _last_pool_track (or index) scheduler slot to avoid back-to-back repeats. MusicLoop must NOT need changes.

Naming: generated track names must not collide at minute resolution (today's auto_track_name is <vibe>-<style>-YYYYMMDD-HHMM, so 2 generations in one minute overwrite and the pool never grows). Include a short unique/variation suffix so a pool reliably grows to 12 distinct files.
Behavior preserved: /music on --name replay, /music play <name>, gapless handoff, and the off path are unchanged. Rotating an established pool makes no API call (assert this).

Tests (exercise the scheduler domain, not just helpers): pool fills to 12 with each generation using a distinct variation index; the 13th on/next ROTATES (no generate/API call — mock the generator/provider and assert call count 0 on rotation); shuffle never returns the immediately-previous track; a different vibe or style is a separate pool; <12 still generates. Mock the ElevenLabs provider — never hit the real API in tests.

DECIDE the transport: evaluate at least the three candidates in the context (A brief-connect ship, B direct multi-writer O_APPEND append to one vox.log with flock-guarded rotation, C client spool drained by daemon) against the invariants, grounded in the ACTUAL code (read the fdmm logging implementation in src/punt_vox/ first — logging setup, client fallback path, daemon log sink, rotation). INVARIANTS the chosen design MUST satisfy, stated verbatim in the doc: (1) one vox.log receives daemon records AND every client record, including hooks that do no daemon work (Stop/UserPromptSubmit/SubagentStop skip paths, config updates); (2) no hook pays a daemon round-trip on its logging hot path (DES-017); (3) rotation is safe under concurrent writers — no lost or torn lines, no write to a renamed file; (4) log files keep 0600 (cn0p); (5) vox-fallback.log is DELETED outright — no fallback file, no migration/compat/shim/bridge code of any kind (hard design gate); (6) observability stays persistent-file, never stderr-only (DES-046).

If it meets the trigger (3+ states with transitions OR invariants that silent-corrupt on a wrong transition), produce docs/vox-2594-log-rotation.tex modeling writer/rotator states with the invariants in the state schema predicate, one operation schema per transition, and fuzz type-check exit 0 (zspec check). If you judge it does NOT qualify, state the reasons explicitly in the design doc — silence is not an assessment.

Behavior matches DES-039 + the Z model: /music on generates track #1, plays it as soon as ready, and the scheduler-owned PoolFiller sequentially fills the (vibe,style) pool to POOL_SIZE=12 in the background; playback AUTO-ADVANCES on each natural track-end with NO command; at 12 the fill task exits and playback rotates (shuffle-avoid-last) with zero provider calls; vibe change finishes the current song then switches pool; /music off cancels the fill synchronously and stops playback; restart resumes from the on-disk count. The single-track transient loops #1 until #2 lands, then advances.

Amendment A implemented: a TrackStore Protocol with a FilesystemTrackStore production impl; all music-domain disk access goes through it; it is constructor-injected through generator -> scheduler/filler -> daemon; an in-memory fake store exists for tests. grep confirms no direct Path.glob / pathlib disk access remains in scheduler.py / filler.py / the generator's domain methods (only FilesystemTrackStore touches the filesystem).

Empty-pool skip no-op: /music next in generating-first returns an ignored/no-op response and never calls pick_next on an empty pool. Regression test asserts this (no raise, no player spawned, fill unaffected).

EXPERIENCE-LEVEL tests (the bas7 gap), driving the REAL loop with a fake subprocess that records each argv and controls when each proc ends: (1) track-end advances to a DIFFERENT on-disk file with no skip_next call and no generate call; (2) PoolFiller fills to 12 then STOPS (exactly POOL_SIZE-N generate calls, task done not cancelled); (3) full pool rotates with generate_track.assert_not_called() across >=2 advances, consecutive tracks differ; (4) vibe change does NOT kill the current player, retargets fill to the new pool immediately, switches playback to a new-pool file at natural end; (5) restart: 5-on-disk -> 7 gens then stop, 12-on-disk -> 0 gens rotating; (6) /music off cancels the fill task (cancelled(), frozen generate count, player killed); (7) single-track loops then advances when #2 lands. Plus test_store.py for the fake + FilesystemTrackStore. Every new module has a test file; async/boundary paths (proc-end, task cancellation) are exercised via asyncio.Event handshakes, not sleep-polling.

DESIGN (hint-based, do NOT re-litigate — DES-045 finalized by leader): vibe() stays a PURE voice-mood tool. It does NOT drive music, gains NO music params, and NO re-pool logic. Its RETURN is enriched, READ-ONLY: it reads the current music Program status and, when a Program is playing (mode != off), includes both the music state (playing, style) AND an IMPERATIVE directive string telling the agent to act — e.g. `music_hint`: 'Music is playing (style=flamenco). Author 12 rich flamenco x <mood> prompts and call the music tool: music(mode=on, style=flamenco, base_prompt=..., variations=[12]). Do it now.' When music is OFF, no hint (music_hint absent/null). vibe() reading status is read-only observation, not control — keep the layering clean (the vibe tool never posts a music/switch signal).

The music RE-POOL itself is UNCHANGED: the existing music tool (music on style ...) already re-pools via SwitchProgram -> turn_on. Do NOT modify the daemon/state machine. The agent, prompted by the vibe() return hint, calls the existing music tool. This is orchestration, not a new transition (no z-spec).

commands/vibe.md (the /vibe skill): add the author-then-call-music step — after calling vibe(), if the return's music_hint is present, author 12 rich genre-accurate prompts for (new mood x current style) — the mood COLORS the genre (relaxing flamenco = slow soleá/guajira; intense flamenco = fast bulerías) — and call the music tool with base_prompt + variations. If no hint (music off), do nothing music-wise. Mirror the /music prompt-authoring rules. This same instruction must apply on the AUTO-vibe path (whenever the agent sets the vibe from its own read of the mood), not just manual /vibe.

OBSERVABILITY IS A CORE DELIVERABLE (operator: 'make that part of the core design goal'). Emit structured, greppable log events (a stable prefix, e.g. `[vibe-trace]`, via logger — voxd/server/hooks log to stderr, never print) at each link so a human can PROVE each feature works or catch it silently failing: (a) AUTO-VIBE: hooks.py handle_vibe_nudge logs each nudge fire with turn/counter/mode ('[vibe-trace] nudge fired counter=5->0 mode=auto'); (b) VIBE SET: server.py vibe() logs mood/tags/mode/music_playing/style/hint_emitted ('[vibe-trace] vibe set mood=relaxing mode=auto music_playing=true style=flamenco hint=emitted'); (c) MUSIC RE-POOL: server.py music() logs the re-pool with style/vibe and existing-rotate vs new-generate ('[vibe-trace] music repool style=flamenco vibe=relaxing pool=new->generate'). The three together let you grep and PROVE: auto-vibe = nudge-fire followed by a vibe-set (mode=auto); a nudge with NO following vibe-set = auto-vibe NOT working. vibe->music = a vibe-set with music_playing=true followed by a music repool; a playing vibe-set with NO repool = the agent dropped the follow-up. Document the exact grep commands (grep '[vibe-trace]' <voxd log path>) in commands/vibe.md or a short note so the operator can run the proof.

Also surface current state through the API, not only logs (per 'client-observable, not logs'): the status tool should reflect the current vibe + current music program so a client can query state; the [vibe-trace] LOG is the event-trail proof-over-time, the status is the point-in-time client-observable state. Keep this bounded — do not build a telemetry system.

Reconcile commands/music.md + the /music skill wording to the actual behavior (a vibe/style change re-pools ONLY while music is playing; music-off vibe change is a no-op; no confirmation/guard). Use the exact wording from the design note (.tmp/missions/results/vox-q1z4-design-note.md docs section) as the starting point, adjusted for the hint-based flow.

Tests: (server) vibe() returns music_hint + imperative directive when status.mode != off; returns NO hint when off; the hint names the current style; vibe() posts NO music/switch signal (layering). (hooks) the nudge logs the [vibe-trace] event with the counter. Assert the [vibe-trace] log lines are emitted at each link (caplog). Do NOT test the LLM's follow-up (that's the soft path, live-verified) — test that vibe() EMITS the hint and the logs fire.

NO suppressions (# noqa, # type: ignore, xfail) — fix the code; the only allowed classes are the PY-DP-9 enumerations with the rule cited inline.

Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (file group, single concern, or single PR-equivalent slice). Each commit must pass make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes. Branch: fix/vox-2594-unified-vox-log (already checked out — do not create branches or worktrees, do not open a PR, do not touch CHANGELOG.md/README.md/DESIGN.md — the leader owns those).

After the code is committed: make install, then vox daemon restart, then make test against the installed artifact (a stale daemon serves the old sink). Report the actual test output in your result note. Do NOT perform the live-verify session or the user-dir stale-file cleanup — the leader runs those with the operator.

Write the result note to .tmp/missions/results/vox-2594-impl-note.md (what changed, files_changed, actual make check + make test output tails typed as text you read in full, any deviation from the design doc with reason) and submit via ethos mission result. Time-box: 30 minutes.

DECIDE the audio-return transport for record. Read the ACTUAL code first (the record request/response path: client.py record + transport send_and_recv, client_sync.py, __main__.py record command, voxd speech_handlers record handler, and how the daemon frames its reply). Evaluate at least these candidates against the invariants: (A) chunked/streamed audio frames with client-side reassembly; (B) raise the negotiated websockets max_size on the audio channel to a bounded ceiling; (C) daemon writes the file itself and returns the PATH (record produces a file anyway; the daemon already owns cache/track/ephemeral files on disk) so large audio never crosses the wire; (D) binary frames instead of JSON-embedded audio. Pick one, document rejection rationale for the others. A different, better alternative is allowed if it satisfies every invariant. State the trust boundary you are designing to (voxd clients are local same-user; browser-origin handshakes already rejected) so the chosen mechanism does not reintroduce an unbounded-buffer exposure.

The design MUST fix all four observed defects (reproduced live 2026-07-20, evidence in the bead): (1) TRANSPORT root cause — a record whose MP3 exceeds ~1 MiB is undeliverable (client closes 1009 message-too-big; 6000-char text = 3,072,047-byte frame); (2) TIMEOUT — a fresh 6000-char ElevenLabs synthesis took 2m04s but the client response timeout expired first, so the daemon finished and cached with no receiver and the -o file was never written (the fix must ensure a legitimate long synthesis is not abandoned — ack-then-stream, length-scaled timeout, or progress frames — state which); (3) CLI ERROR MAPPING — websockets ConnectionClosedError escapes __main__.py record`s (VoxdConnectionError, VoxdProtocolError) catch as a raw rich traceback; every transport failure must surface as a one-line CLI error (CLI standard); (4) DAEMON-SIDE — when the client disconnects mid-transfer the daemon raises AssertionError in the websockets drain path (uvicorn.error: data transfer failed); the daemon must handle a client close gracefully and never leave its send path in a bad state.

INVARIANTS the chosen design MUST satisfy, stated verbatim in the doc: (1) vox record of arbitrarily long text delivers the COMPLETE, byte-correct MP3 to the requested output path (assert reassembled/written bytes == synthesized bytes); (2) no silent size ceiling — either no single-frame cap on audio or a transfer with no practical size limit within the trust boundary; (3) a legitimate long synthesis is never abandoned by a premature client timeout; (4) all client-facing transport failures are one-line CLI errors, never a traceback; (5) a client disconnecting mid-transfer never crashes the daemon or corrupts its connection/send state; (6) no unbounded-buffer exposure beyond the stated local-same-user trust boundary; (7) no migration/compat/shim/bridge code — forward-integrate the old single-frame return path out.

Z-SPEC ASSESSMENT (required per the CLAUDE.md formal-modeling gate). If the chosen transport is a stateful transfer (e.g. chunked streaming with reassembly: idle -> transferring -> complete/aborted, with invariants like in-order delivery, reassembled == produced, abort leaves no partial file) it meets the trigger — produce docs/vox-tnum-record-transport.tex modeling the transfer states with invariants in the state schema predicate, one operation schema per transition, fuzz -t exit 0 (zspec check). If the chosen approach is NOT a state machine (e.g. candidate C path-return, or a single bounded-size frame), state the reasons explicitly in the design doc — silence is not an assessment.

The design doc docs/vox-tnum-record-transport.md contains: chosen approach + mechanism detail (message/frame schema, ordering/reassembly or path-handoff protocol, timeout/ack behavior, failure behavior on client-close and on synthesis error); a note on whether the SAME return path is used by any other client call that returns bytes (e.g. does any play/segment path or a future consumer hit the same ceiling) and whether they are fixed by the same change; the IMPLEMENTATION WRITE SET (files to create/modify/delete — your decision, not inherited); a test plan whose cases assert the invariants BY NAME (e.g. "long record delivers byte-correct file", "record over frame limit succeeds", "client-close mid-transfer does not crash daemon", "transport failure is a one-line CLI error"); and a LIVE-VERIFY plan for the running machine (drive a real >1 MiB record end-to-end; the -o file is written and plays back; a mid-transfer disconnect leaves the daemon healthy per vox doctor). Also RULE on the secondary observation: record log lines show voice=' (provider default used) while the session voice is roger — decide whether vox record should inherit the session voice like say/unmute, and if so include it in the write set; if not, justify.

Commit the design artifacts to branch fix/vox-tnum-record-transport (already checked out). make check green before the commit (read FULL output; run gates individually if the combined target stalls). Do NOT write implementation code, do NOT touch src/, do NOT open a PR.
Write the result summary to .tmp/missions/results/vox-tnum-design-note.md and submit via ethos mission result. Time-box: single focused design pass, target 20-25 minutes.

Implement docs/vox-tnum-record-transport.md exactly — candidate C: the daemon writes the synthesized MP3 to the caller-specified destination and returns {type:audio, path, bytes}; NO audio crosses the WebSocket. Fix all four defects the doc enumerates: (1) big-frame 1009 (gone — no payload frame); (2) premature timeout (immediate `recording` ack + length-scaled client wait for the terminal `audio` frame); (3) CLI raw traceback (transport wraps websockets ConnectionClosed/WebSocketException and OSError from ws.recv()/ws.send() into VoxdConnectionError/VoxdProtocolError — the general fix for every call, not just record); (4) daemon AssertionError on client mid-transfer close (guard the reply with contextlib.suppress(WebSocketDisconnect, RuntimeError), matching SynthesizeHandler).

Atomic destination write: synthesize to a sibling temp in the destination directory, then os.replace onto the final path, so a crash or synthesis error mid-write leaves NO partial file. Cache hit = copy from the cache file (never move — preserve the cache entry); fresh synthesis = move the ephemeral temp. Preserve 0600/private semantics the existing sink uses.

Forward integration, no shim: delete src/punt_vox/recording.py (RecordingSink) and tests/test_recording.py; file-write + content-hash naming (generate_filename) move daemon-side (record_sink.py). VoxClient.record changes shape from returning bytes to returning a RecordResult(path, bytes, cached); every caller (CLI record, MCP record tool) changes with it. No base64 path remains.

OUT OF SCOPE — do NOT implement here (separate beads): remote host:path labeling and cross-host path resolution (vox-eoq9, P4); routing vox play through the daemon (vox-ovb7, P3); the record/say session-voice inheritance fix (its own later mission — it touches synthesis.py + server.py and must not collide with this mission). Implement C for the local/loopback case the doc specifies; the daemon writes to the output_path/output_dir it is given.

Tests assert the invariants BY NAME per the design doc test plan: test_long_record_delivers_byte_correct_file (output size == reply bytes == source len); test_record_over_frame_limit_succeeds (terminal frame is a path frame, no data key, no >1MiB frame); test_arbitrarily_long_record_has_no_ceiling; test_long_synthesis_is_not_abandoned (fake synth sleeps past the old 30s; client still gets audio under the length-scaled deadline); test_client_close_mid_transfer_does_not_crash_daemon (disconnect after ack before audio; handler returns clean; a subsequent health on a new connection succeeds); test_transport_failure_is_one_line_cli_error (force ConnectionClosedError; wrapped as VoxdProtocolError; CLI exits 1, single line, no traceback); test_no_partial_file_on_synthesis_error (synth raises after ack; destination does NOT exist).

Design the coherent "daemon is the audio host" model as ONE change, resolving three linked problems: (P1 SECURITY, vox-zu39) voxd currently writes/replaces any client-supplied ABSOLUTE output_path/output_dir as the daemon OS user — RecordHandler only checks is_absolute, RecordSink mkdirs + os.replace. Across the remote trust boundary (machine B holds A's VOXD_TOKEN per docs/guide-remote-setup.md), a compromised B gets arbitrary file overwrite on A -> likely RCE. (vox-ovb7) `vox play <file>` runs the player in the CLIENT process (playback.py play_audio, no daemon) so it plays on the wrong machine remotely. (vox-eoq9) a recording lands on the daemon host but the client has no coherent way to reference, play, or retrieve it. Read the ACTUAL code first (record_handler, record_sink, playback, client.record, the __main__ record/play commands, client_gateway).

SECURITY INVARIANT the design MUST establish (this is the P1): the daemon NEVER writes to a client-dictated absolute path. Record output resolves under a daemon-owned recordings root; the client sends a filename or a generated id (or nothing -> daemon-generated content-addressed name); the daemon rejects absolute paths and traversal (..), resolves the candidate, and verifies containment within the root before writing. The token authorizes audio operations, not filesystem writes as the daemon user. Forward-integrate the #351 output_path/output_dir absolute-path wire contract (no shim). State the trust model explicitly in the design.

DECIDE the local-vs-remote coherence — the crux, and the main thing to escalate. Locally (loopback daemon) `vox record -o ./out.mp3` today lands the file where the user expects on their one machine; that ergonomic must be preserved or a clearly-better replacement designed. Remotely the file lives under the daemon root and is referenced/played/retrieved through the daemon. Options to weigh and pick among (or better): (a) uniform — daemon-owned store always, client refers by id, `-o` becomes a client-side retrieval (stream bytes back to the client path) rather than a daemon-side write path; (b) local fast-path — loopback keeps direct client-path writes, remote is sandboxed+id-based; (c) always stream bytes back to the client and the client writes locally (this removes the daemon-side write primitive entirely and makes record client-local again, at the cost of re-adding a bytes-over-wire path — reconcile with why #351 moved it daemon-side). Document the rejected options and WHY.

PLAY through the daemon (vox-ovb7): design a wire op so `vox play` plays on the daemon host (where the user hears audio), referencing a file by the daemon-owned id/name from the record store — NOT a client absolute path (same trust constraint). Preserve the current local behavior (loopback: plays on the one machine). The flock playback serialization must still hold.

RETRIEVAL + labeling (vox-eoq9): how a remote client learns where its recording is and gets it to B if wanted (stream-back or explicit fetch), and how record output is reported (an id and/or host:path locator, only when remote; bare path when local). No exploit-y absolute-path echoing.

Z-SPEC / formal-modeling assessment (required per CLAUDE.md). Assess whether the record-store containment + play/retrieve protocol qualifies (state + safety invariant "every write stays within the root", "no client path escapes the sandbox"). If it qualifies produce docs/vox-dvri-record-store.tex modeling it with the containment invariant in the state schema, fuzz -t clean; if not, state why explicitly.

The design doc docs/vox-dvri-daemon-audio-host.md contains: the chosen model + mechanism (record-store root layout, id scheme, containment check, wire ops for record/play/retrieve, what changes in the #351 contract); the security analysis (the closed primitive, the trust model, the test that proves containment); the IMPLEMENTATION WRITE SET (your decision — likely record_handler, record_sink, a new store module, playback/play wire op, client, __main__, server, and the guide + DESIGN ADR); a test plan asserting the invariants BY NAME (e.g. "wire absolute path rejected", "traversal rejected", "write cannot escape the root", "play routes through the daemon", "local record keeps its path ergonomic"); a LIVE-VERIFY plan; and how the guide (docs/guide-remote-setup.md) record/play section gets rewritten once this lands (removing the current caveats). Note the related-but-separate mechanical beads vox-4p5p (daemon status via client.health) and vox-suvs (cache via daemon) are OUT of this design — they are their own quick fixes.

Commit the design artifacts to branch feat/vox-dvri-design (already checked out in this worktree). make check green before the commit (run gates individually if the combined target stalls; read full output). Do NOT write implementation code, do NOT touch src/, do NOT open a PR.
Write the result to .tmp/missions/results/vox-dvri-design-note.md and submit via ethos mission result. Time-box a focused pass, ~25-30 min. Decisive and implementable.

STEP 1 — amend docs/vox-dvri-daemon-audio-host.md to the operator-ratified PURE model (2026-07-21), then implement it. Pure model: ONE philosophy, three orthogonal verbs. `vox record` has NO `-o`/output/name-as-path client path and does NO client-side delivery — it captures to the daemon store and returns/prints an id + store locator (the store path when local, a `<name> on <host>` locator when remote). Materialization to a caller-named path is `vox fetch <id> -o <path>` ONLY — the sole verb that writes bytes to a client path. Remove the shared-filesystem delivery probe from the record path entirely (record carries zero path/trust surface). `vox play <ARG>` dispatch is unchanged: an existing local file plays client-side (loopback, no trust crossing); otherwise ARG is a store ref played on the daemon host. Update the doc's client-surface and guide-rewrite sections to match. Keep docs/vox-dvri-record-store.tex (the containment Z spec) in sync if the model shifts; it is already fuzz -t clean.

SECURITY INVARIANT (the P1, vox-zu39, non-negotiable): the daemon never writes, plays, or reads a client-dictated path. For record (bare optional name or generated content-addressed id), play (ref), and fetch (ref): reject a candidate that is absolute, contains a separator (/ or \\), contains a .. component, is empty, or contains a NUL — with an error frame, before any filesystem touch; resolve under the daemon-owned 0700 recordings root; verify resolved.is_relative_to(root.resolve()) AFTER .resolve() (so symlink/.. normalization cannot smuggle an escape); write via the existing mkstemp-fd atomic primitive inside the root. The token authorizes audio operations, not filesystem writes/reads as the daemon user. Peer address (loopback vs not) is NOT an input to the security decision.

Implement per the design write set: NEW voxd/record_store.py (owns the containment invariant: resolve(name,text)->Path, place(source,text,name,cached)->RecordWrite atomic in-root, resolve_ref(ref)->Path; supersedes record_sink.py), NEW voxd/play_handler.py (resolve ref in-root, enqueue on the serialized PlaybackQueue, ack playing — plays on the daemon host), NEW voxd/fetch_handler.py (resolve ref in-root, single-frame base64 bytes; large remote fetch >frame-limit is NOT supported this cut — documented, no working behaviour lost per docs/vox-tnum-record-transport.md). DELETE voxd/record_sink.py + its test and the #351 output_dir/output_path wire fields (forward integration, no shim). Modify record_handler (bare-name validation over RecordStore; terminal audio frame carries name/store path/bytes/cached), handler_registry (register play + fetch; wire shared RecordStore + PlaybackQueue), paths.py (recordings_dir(), created 0700 in ensure_user_dirs), client.py/client_sync.py (record signature drops output_dir/output_path, RecordResult=(id,name,store_path,byte_count,cached); add play(ref) and fetch(ref)->bytes), __main__.py (record prints locator/store path, NO -o; play dispatch; NEW `vox fetch <ref> -o <path>` command), server.py (record tool adapts to the new RecordResult). fetch owns any local-copy-vs-wire delivery detail; record does not.

Tests assert the invariants BY NAME (design test plan): test_wire_absolute_path_rejected, test_wire_traversal_rejected, test_wire_separator_in_name_rejected, test_write_cannot_escape_root (property test over hostile names: absolute, .. at every position, embedded NUL, symlink-shaped), test_token_does_not_grant_fs_write, test_play_ref_outside_root_rejected, test_fetch_ref_outside_root_rejected; plus coherence: test_default_name_is_content_addressed, test_play_routes_through_daemon (enqueues the in-root path on the daemon PlaybackQueue, NOT client-side), test_remote_record_returns_locator, test_remote_fetch_delivers_bytes. Use the flat tests/test_voxd_*.py layout that exists in this repo (the tests/voxd/* write_set paths map to tests/test_voxd_*.py).
